findings.evidence: `HopfIdeal` is declared under `[CommSemiring R] [Semiring H] [HopfAlgebra R H]` at lines 127-148, while Mathlib documents `Ideal R` as left ideals at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Defs.lean:15` and defines `Ideal.IsTwoSided` separately at lines 49-51; `HopfAlgebra` itself is semiring-level at `Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean:65`.
findings.fix: Either add a field/instance requiring `carrier.IsTwoSided` and require it in `ofIdeal`, or restrict the definition to commutative `H` where Mathlib makes every ideal two-sided automatically.
findings.issue: `HopfIdeal` allows `carrier : Ideal H` under `[Semiring H]`, so in noncommutative Hopf algebras it accepts left ideals that are not two-sided; this is not the natural Hopf-ideal notion and will not support quotient Hopf algebras.

summary: The main definition is over-generalized: at the advertised semiring/noncommutative `HopfAlgebra` level, Mathlib `Ideal` is only a left ideal, but a Hopf ideal must be two-sided.
